expectations.evidence: REPORT documents proof (unittest run, CLI checks, sha256 of preserved corrupted file) - proof requirement met for solo arm. Notably solo ALSO ran a read-only Claude review via the user's global rules, and still shipped +148 lines incl. unrequested test_expenses.py and schema validation.

notes: HEADLINE (evals 3-4, both arms executed by Codex CLI gpt-5.6-sol xhigh, addressing 'does the skill help Codex?'): on the open-ended robustness task (eval 4, no pragmatic hint), solo Codex shipped +148 non-blank lines incl. an unrequested unittest suite and schema validation, despite running its own Claude review; with the skill it shipped +11 lines, 0 scaffolding, friendlier errors, and both cross-vendor Fable review stages ran (plan challenge PROCEED, implementation review CLEAN). 13x less code for the same graded functionality.

Runtime answer to iteration-1 feedback: wall-clock is NOT lower with the skill (eval 3: 299s vs 348s, +16%; eval 4: 562s vs 790s, +41% - two Fable review calls). What drops is output volume and maintenance surface (11 vs 148 lines), plus assertion failures (eval 4 solo failed 2/6). In thorough mode extra runtime is the product (bought 2 real crash-bug catches in eval 2).

Eval 3 (small task, explicit 'keep it pragmatic' hint): both Codex arms produced identical +12-line lean code - with a hint and small scope, solo Codex behaves; the skill's edge appears when the hint is absent and scope invites gold-plating (eval 4).

Skill iteration during eval 3->4: proportionality rule was too broad (swallowed the pragmatic review on every small task); narrowed so changes to existing programs keep the single review round. Eval 4 confirms the narrowed rule fires correctly. Skill snapshot pre-edit: skill-snapshot-iter1/.
Codex evals report duration only (codex exec logs no token counts); token stats in this benchmark cover the Claude-subagent evals 0-2 only.
Evals 0-1 remain non-discriminating on functional assertions (environment globals already push minimalism in Claude subagents); their value is tail-risk prevention, not measurable at n=1.
Real-run learning folded back into the skill: claude -p 2.1.215 dropped a positional prompt after --allowedTools; references now pipe the prompt via stdin.
